prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a geometric Brownian motion model, a common approach for stock price forecasting. For this model, an annualized expected return of 8% and an annualized volatility of 28% were assumed. These are illustrative parameters, reflecting historical averages for large-cap stocks, as specific real-time NKE data for these metrics is not available. The point estimate represents the median of the projected log-normal price distribution. The confidence intervals are calculated using the derived standard deviation of log returns over the 21-day period and standard Z-scores corresponding to each confidence level. It is crucial to understand that this model relies on statistical assumptions and does not incorporate real-time market news, company-specific events, or current market sentiment, all of which can significantly influence actual short-term price movements.
